tweets.text: .@SecBlinken discussing Iran on @FacetheNation today: “We still believe diplomacy is the best path forward.” Full transcript: https://t.co/aIBh13GYrM.
.@SecBlinken appeared on @CNNSotu where he discussed #COP26, the Taiwan Relations Act, Iran, and Afghanistan. Read more in the transcript: https://t.co/oiLDIMQqF0.
.@POTUS holds a press conference from Rome, Italy.

.@COP26 must be the turning point – the moment when the world commits to the actions necessary to reach net-zero emissions. Read more in the @WhiteHouse Fact Sheet on Climate: https://t.co/oHoGGFA5Ei.
.@POTUS discusses investments to help tackle the climate crisis through green infrastructure and electric vehicles -- which will also create good-paying jobs for workers. https://t.co/BR9U5o0aW2
We wish all trick-or-treaters around the world a safe and fun Halloween. https://t.co/CXrduFVW78

The United States and Holy See are working together to raise countries’ collective ambition to address the climate crisis, including by raising and implementing national emissions reductions targets. https://t.co/jFTqeQBo0Q

Reaching net-zero emissions is the greatest economic opportunity of our time. Nations that work together to invest in a greener and cleaner economy will create better lives for all their citizens. Learn more about U.S. Climate Action: https://t.co/JjHSOm4btG https://t.co/k8rTkPOkIQ

Department HR Specialist Arisa Nakamori, Senior Policy Advisor Mariyam Cementwala, and Special Assistant Brian Taylor speak on disability being a part of diversity and showing others that they are included. #NDEAM https://t.co/6Nt8iL5ZOt

The United States and Holy See share a common commitment to the fight against human trafficking. The Holy See is a valued partner in the global effort to prevent this heinous crime. https://t.co/uWo8vuRB1Z

Today the U.S. delivered over 850,000 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to the people of Morocco, in addition to $2 million for urgent COVID-19 assistance. We are working with our global partners and @gavi #COVAX to deliver safe and effective vaccines around the world. https://t.co/XMftFViBj7

Our relationship with Italy plays an important role in guaranteeing Transatlantic security, marshaling support for progress in Libya, and addressing shared threats in the Mediterranean region and around the world. https://t.co/Cs77klNYNN

The U.S. delivered 3.6 million doses of the Pfizer vaccine to the people of Egypt, bringing the total doses delivered over the past month to 8.25 million. We are working with our global partners and @gavi #COVAX to deliver safe and effective vaccines around the world.

More than 20 million Americans proudly claim Italian heritage. Throughout our history, Italian-Americans have greatly enriched the fabric of American life. Read more: https://t.co/XNTH3ccRyQ. https://t.co/ohBfbhVkO6

The G20 enables the United States to engage with the world’s largest economies to address global economic, political, and social issues. This includes combating the COVID-19 pandemic, tackling the climate crisis, and fostering economic recovery. https://t.co/ieaE0NLFaZ

Today the United States delivered 990,990 additional doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Kenya via #COVAX. To date, we have provided nearly 4 million safe and effective vaccine doses to support Kenya's nationwide vaccine rollout program, all with no strings attached. https://t.co/YeB8IYx2Nc

As the United States continues to lead global efforts to combat COVID-19, we are proud to announce the delivery of 250,040 doses of the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine to Honduras. To date, we have provided nearly 3.9 million vaccine doses to the people of Honduras through #COVAX. https://t.co/IH7KUG2hTt

As a blind, Muslim, first-generation American, Sara Minkara has a lifetime of experience advocating for equity, accessibility, inclusion, and empowerment. Join us in welcoming her as she takes on her new role as the Special Advisor on International Disability Rights. https://t.co/OmFTWpFZ88

Smart investments in infrastructure and innovation can build a zero-carbon economy. Tim Sippey shares his story of helping to build "a small little pod on this giant planet." Little parts can add up to big differences in beating the #ClimateCrisis. https://t.co/Z19SiEDTkg

In the October edition of Science Speaks, @StateGWI’s Dr. Aubrey Paris shares how the fictional story of Cinderella can motivate us to improve the reality of air quality for all, and especially women and members of marginalized communities. Have a read: https://t.co/UTJiJ1fMX3.

The U.S. values its cooperation with the Holy See and Catholic organizations on the ground to deliver humanitarian aid successfully and efficiently. Through our diplomatic relations with the Holy See, the United States benefits from its unparalleled global presence. https://t.co/K71ExbTYT7

The United States is Italy’s largest export market outside the EU. Our two-way trade in goods and services amounted to $80 billion last year. Our relationship has spurred collaboration in fields outside the economic realm, including security and space technology. https://t.co/yuf3Jodane

The #IndoPacificBizForum advances the U.S.’s vision for an open, interconnected, resilient & secure Indo-Pacific region. Together, #IndoPacific businesses and governments are supporting economic recovery, climate action, and digital innovation. More: https://t.co/JLiM0hPj1z.

Department Senior Advisor for Employee Communications Roberta Mather: For the Department to succeed, we need diverse thinkers who see the world with different viewpoints. When we bring all of that here, that becomes the core of diplomacy. #NDEAM https://t.co/mITsyioZFo

October is #CybersecurityAwarenessMonth. Deputy Secretary Brian P. McKeon outlines how we are working with partners and allies to advance security in cyberspace and deter cybercriminals and hold them accountable. Learn more at https://t.co/rvKEAuiyxu. https://t.co/0GqoN3dlH5

The United States is proud to announce our delivery of 305,370 doses of safe, effective Pfizer COVID-19 vaccines to the people of Nicaragua via our #COVAX multilateral partnership. We are committed to supporting Nicaragua and the region as we work together toward recovery. https://t.co/2CSCXFlloe

@StateINR Brandon Strassberg of @StateDRL: Having a workforce that represents the American public, both domestically and overseas, I think is a pivotal piece of promoting disability rights. #NDEAM https://t.co/RjMgfxK8s5

On his first day, @SecBlinken made a commitment to leave behind a State Department ready to meet the tests of the 21st century. Read how he is making good on that commitment in our latest DipNote: https://t.co/dNhdJDQsoV.
.@SecBlinken on the modernization of the State Department: We will elevate new voices and foster a climate of initiative and innovation. https://t.co/habHU34pKH
.@SecBlinken: We will build and retain a diverse, dynamic, and entrepreneurial workforce – and we will equip and empower our employees to succeed. https://t.co/tEs9tBC3O0

.@SecBlinken: Ultimately, migration is about people wanting a better life for themselves & their families. Together we can help make that happen both by making migration safe, orderly, humane & by laying a foundation for more inclusive growth & opportunity across the hemisphere. https://t.co/i9V1hqnlXS

The United States is proud to work with #COVAX to deliver 2.6 million additional doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to our partner Vietnam. We have provided a total of 12.1 million vaccine doses to Vietnam, with no strings attached, to help combat the pandemic. https://t.co/s1C4gepvny

As part of our vaccination efforts in Central Asia and beyond, the U.S. is proud to deliver 259,740 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to the people of the Kyrgyz Republic. Together, we are saving lives and building a world that is safe from the threat of infectious disease. https://t.co/RBalMneQX0

Investing in clean energy and promoting a net-zero U.S. and global economy is the way out of the #ClimateCrisis. We are rallying other countries in this critical moment to join us. We are not sitting this one out. https://t.co/GREPYmBNU1
.@SecBlinken discusses modernizing the U.S. Department of State’s tech, comms, and analytical capabilities to confront and solve foreign policy challenges. https://t.co/vP2udljfXK

.@SecBlinken: If we’re not engaged in international institutions, then we leave a void likely to be filled by others who may not share our values and interests –  or no one steps up and we squander the benefits of collective action. https://t.co/zeF3Aoywmy

.@SecBlinken on the modernization of the State Department: "We need to do more on diversity, inclusion, accessibility, and equity, so talented people from all walks of life can see the State Department as a place where they belong and can thrive." https://t.co/CrXG6ap3jF

To mark 20 years of the @GilmanProgram, @ECAatState has announced the Top Producing Institutions of Gilman Scholars over the program’s history. We thank all institutions working to make study abroad more accessible to American students. See the list: https://t.co/IZGIUNDD2g. https://t.co/XyfVQPkw6X

Three years ago today, 11 people were killed during the terrorist attack on the Tree of Life Jewish synagogue in Pittsburgh. At the recent @EradicateHate_ Summit, @StateDeptCT discussed countering antisemitic hate at home and globally. Read more here: https://t.co/3fSuUKqyU9.

.@SecBlinken: I committed on my first day in office to leave behind a State Department ready to meet the tests of the 21st century – an even stronger, more effective, more agile, more diverse institution that can lead America's engagement in the world. https://t.co/LeuShOQrLa
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks on modernizing American diplomacy to meet the foreign policy challenges of the 21st century. https://t.co/HHmh7tCN2N

Today at approximately 11:30 a.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will deliver remarks on modernizing American diplomacy to meet the foreign policy challenges of the 21st century. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7, and https://t.co/l1XlxVvn3n. https://t.co/wnTZFyZ4rb

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom is a human right; in fact, it goes to the heart of what it means to be human – to think freely, to follow our conscience, to change our beliefs if our hearts and minds lead us to do so, to express those beliefs in public and in private. https://t.co/tYzeRLD3Ex

Today we mark International Religious Freedom Day. We stand with, and for, all who seek to live out the dictates of their conscience. Freedom of religion or belief is a fundamental freedom. https://t.co/qI7mQ0vLeY

.@StateDeptSpox: Taiwan's exclusion from @UN
 bodies undermines the institution's important work. The United States sees Taiwan as a valued partner, a democratic success story, and a trusted friend that should have meaningful participation in the UN system.

Tune in to watch @SecBlinken deliver remarks on modernizing American diplomacy to meet the foreign policy challenges of the 21st century: Wednesday, October 27 at 11:30 a.m. EDT. More: https://t.co/1gp9xZdhNm. https://t.co/WvQO1IQktI

This year, @HistoryAtState turns 100. In celebration of 100 years of foreign policy history, the Department’s Elizabeth Charles shares her experiences as a professional historian. https://t.co/Yven0i5uoq

.@StateDeptSpox on Intersex Awareness Day: As @POTUS and @SecBlinken have made clear, it is the policy of the United States to pursue an end to violence and discrimination on the basis of gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or sex characteristics. https://t.co/kJPOLhWNch

.@StateDeptSpox provides an update on the U.S.-ASEAN Summit and U.S. commitment to provide up to $102 million in new initiatives, emphasizing our deep commitment to @ASEAN’s central role in preserving a free and open Indo-Pacific. More: https://t.co/5CafDqGmdz. https://t.co/9C6EYmmBeX
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/TxFMuyCBy0

Today at approximately 2 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/HVgQEXRepa. https://t.co/BrvQYIJujS

We are committed to a State Department that is truly representative of the American people. Read our latest DipNote on how the Department is working to bring more deaf people into the Foreign Service and the Department. https://t.co/Jfc3aNby8S
@StateINR State Department IT Specialist Rajiv Shah: Disability is an aspect of diversity, because it demonstrates that people have all sorts of abilities. #NDEAM https://t.co/vEFslSjUdO

.@POTUS: Around the globe, including here at home, brave lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, and intersex (LGBTQI+) activists are fighting for equal protection under the law, freedom from violence, and recognition of their fundamental human rights. https://t.co/E72oFbXfg6

Today on Intersex Awareness Day, we recognize the voices of intersex people around the world. Here at the Department, we are committed to promoting and protecting the human rights of intersex persons. Learn more here: https://t.co/st0fqTddQf.

The United States is proud to announce the delivery of 336,000 additional doses of the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine to the people of Madagascar. By providing vaccines to our friends and allies around the globe, we are helping to build a safer and more secure world. https://t.co/gKNMRbUrxp

The United States delivered 800,280 additional doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Uzbekistan this month, with over 5 million doses provided to Uzbekistan in total. We will continue to partner with the people of Uzbekistan to save lives and end the COVID-19 pandemic. https://t.co/PeqfkVnu82

The U.S. is committed to working with our global partners to provide safe and effective vaccines. Today we delivered 100,620 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Nepal. We are proud to have now provided more than 1.6 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Nepal. https://t.co/yZmOVwfvhw

No matter where you are in the United States, you and your neighbors can help welcome arriving Afghan families through the new @sponsorship_hub Sponsor Circle program for Afghans. Learn how by visiting https://t.co/EkVgbAzejj. https://t.co/o5GGmUmdwC
Beginning November 8, the U.S. will have a new policy for Americans returning home after traveling abroad. Here’s what you need to know before you go: https://t.co/oOT2DnZknX https://t.co/R9gPLwQWwG
